meta.certificationName: Microsoft Certified: Azure AI Apps and Agents Developer Associate (AI-103)
meta.title: MS-AI103-6: Azure AI Foundry Agents, Retrieval, Evaluation, and Responsible AI

domains.name: Azure AI Foundry and Application Development

questions.stem:
A team is building a chat application in Azure AI Foundry and needs a project-level container for model deployments, connections, and evaluations.

What should they create first?

options.text: An Azure AI Search indexer without a search index
A Prompt Shield policy attached to no application
An Azure AI Foundry project in the appropriate hub or resource context
A Document Intelligence custom extractor only

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry projects](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/how-to/create-projects).

**B** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry projects](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/how-to/create-projects).

**C** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Azure AI Foundry projects](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/how-to/create-projects).

**D** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry projects](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/how-to/create-projects).

questions.stem:
A developer must choose between OpenAI, Mistral, Meta, and Microsoft models available through Azure AI Foundry before deployment.

Where should the developer compare and select models?

options.text: The Azure AI Search synonym map page
The Content Safety blocklist editor
The Agent Service thread message log
The Azure AI Foundry model catalog

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry model catalog](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/how-to/model-catalog-overview).

**B** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry model catalog](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/how-to/model-catalog-overview).

**C** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry model catalog](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/how-to/model-catalog-overview).

**D** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Azure AI Foundry model catalog](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/how-to/model-catalog-overview).

questions.stem:
A sales assistant needs persistent instructions, model configuration, and tool access managed as an agent in Azure AI Foundry.

Which service capability matches this design?

options.text: Create an Azure AI Foundry Agent with the required tools and instructions
Create only a vector field in Azure AI Search
Run an evaluation dataset as the live agent
Use Prompt Shields as the orchestration loop

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Azure AI Foundry Agent Service](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/overview).

**B** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry Agent Service](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/overview).

**C** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry Agent Service](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/overview).

**D** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry Agent Service](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/overview).

questions.stem:
In a employee handbook scenario, an HR assistant must answer from uploaded policy documents without the team building its own chunking and retrieval implementation.

Which Agent Service tool should be considered?

options.text: Code interpreter for arbitrary Python execution only
File search for retrieval over uploaded files
Groundedness detection without a retrieval source
A custom model catalog deployment with no files

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry file search tool](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/how-to/tools/file-search).

**B** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Azure AI Foundry file search tool](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/how-to/tools/file-search).

**C** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry file search tool](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/how-to/tools/file-search).

**D** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry file search tool](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/how-to/tools/file-search).

A data-analysis agent must inspect a CSV, run calculations, and produce a chart during the conversation.

Which built-in tool best supports this?

options.text: Prompt Shields
A semantic ranking configuration alone
Code interpreter

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry code interpreter tool](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/how-to/tools/code-interpreter).

**D** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry code interpreter tool](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/how-to/tools/code-interpreter).

questions.stem:
A RAG application stores embeddings in Azure AI Search and must retrieve nearest neighbors by vector similarity.

Which index capability is required?

options.text: Content Safety severity thresholds
Agent thread truncation only
A model deployment quota increase
Vector search fields and vector queries in Azure AI Search

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Vector search in Azure AI Search](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/search/vector-search-overview).

**B** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Vector search in Azure AI Search](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/search/vector-search-overview).

**C** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Vector search in Azure AI Search](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/search/vector-search-overview).

**D** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Vector search in Azure AI Search](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/search/vector-search-overview).

questions.stem:
A legal-search assistant performs poorly when users include exact contract numbers and semantic descriptions together. The team wants keyword and vector signals combined.

Which retrieval approach is most appropriate?

options.text: Hybrid search combining keyword and vector retrieval, with ranking fusion
Only image OCR with no vector index
A larger system prompt instead of retrieval
A code interpreter session for every search

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Hybrid search in Azure AI Search](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/search/hybrid-search-overview).

**B** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Hybrid search in Azure AI Search](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/search/hybrid-search-overview).

**C** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Hybrid search in Azure AI Search](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/search/hybrid-search-overview).

**D** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Hybrid search in Azure AI Search](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/search/hybrid-search-overview).

questions.stem:
A company has PDFs in Azure Blob Storage and wants scheduled crawling into an Azure AI Search index.

Which component performs the data-source crawl?

options.text: A Prompt Shield detector
An Azure AI Search indexer
An Agent Service action group
A model evaluation threshold

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Indexers in Azure AI Search](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/search/search-indexer-overview).

**B** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Indexers in Azure AI Search](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/search/search-indexer-overview).

**C** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Indexers in Azure AI Search](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/search/search-indexer-overview).

**D** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Indexers in Azure AI Search](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/search/search-indexer-overview).

questions.stem:
A consumer-facing app must detect harmful user text and reject categories above configured severity levels.

Which Azure AI service should be integrated?

options.text: Azure AI Search semantic captions
Agent file search
Azure AI Content Safety
Foundry project connections only

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Content Safety](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/content-safety/overview).

**B** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Content Safety](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/content-safety/overview).

**C** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Azure AI Content Safety](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/content-safety/overview).

**D** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Content Safety](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/content-safety/overview).

questions.stem:
A RAG assistant reads web pages supplied by users, and attackers may hide instructions in those pages to override the system prompt.

Which protection is designed for this risk?

options.text: Batch scoring in Azure Machine Learning
Semantic captions in AI Search only
A larger context window
Prompt Shields for jailbreak and indirect prompt injection detection

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Prompt Shields](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/content-safety/concepts/jailbreak-detection).

**B** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Prompt Shields](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/content-safety/concepts/jailbreak-detection).

**C** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Prompt Shields](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/content-safety/concepts/jailbreak-detection).

**D** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Prompt Shields](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/content-safety/concepts/jailbreak-detection).

questions.stem:
In a employee handbook scenario, before release, a team wants to measure groundedness, relevance, and safety of generated answers against a test dataset.

Which Azure AI Foundry activity should they run?

options.text: A generative AI evaluation using quality and safety evaluators
A content blocklist as the only quality metric
A vector indexer schedule with no test prompts
A model catalog deployment deletion

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Evaluate generative AI applications](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/concepts/evaluation-approach-gen-ai).

**B** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Evaluate generative AI applications](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/concepts/evaluation-approach-gen-ai).

**C** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Evaluate generative AI applications](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/concepts/evaluation-approach-gen-ai).

**D** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Evaluate generative AI applications](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/concepts/evaluation-approach-gen-ai).

questions.stem:
A support agent conversation is growing long and the model begins losing earlier details. The developer needs to manage retained messages and tool outputs deliberately.

Which design consideration is most relevant?

options.text: Increasing Azure AI Search replicas only
Conversation thread context management for the agent and model context window
Replacing all prompts with image inputs
Disabling tool calls to avoid state

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry Agent Service](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/overview).

**B** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Azure AI Foundry Agent Service](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/overview).

**C** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry Agent Service](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/overview).

**D** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry Agent Service](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/overview).

questions.stem:
A finance assistant must always state that outputs are estimates and must use a retrieval tool before answering policy questions.

Where should this durable behavior be expressed?

options.text: In an indexer schedule name
In Content Safety severity labels only
In the agent instructions or system-level prompt configuration
In the user message after each response

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry Agent tools](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/how-to/tools/overview).

**B** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry Agent tools](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/how-to/tools/overview).

**C** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Azure AI Foundry Agent tools](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/how-to/tools/overview).

**D** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry Agent tools](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/how-to/tools/overview).

questions.stem:
A team is building an evaluation set for a RAG application and needs representative prompts with expected grounding context.

What should the dataset support?

options.text: Only endpoint uptime checks
Only vector index storage size comparisons
Only model catalog screenshots
Evaluation of generated answers against expected quality and grounding criteria

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Evaluate generative AI applications](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/concepts/evaluation-approach-gen-ai).

**B** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Evaluate generative AI applications](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/concepts/evaluation-approach-gen-ai).

**C** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Evaluate generative AI applications](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/concepts/evaluation-approach-gen-ai).

**D** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Evaluate generative AI applications](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/concepts/evaluation-approach-gen-ai).

questions.stem:
A tutoring agent must preserve student preferences across turns while avoiding uncontrolled growth of raw chat history.

Which pattern is most appropriate?

options.text: Store durable preferences separately and inject only relevant context into the agent conversation
Append every historical message forever to each prompt
Disable threads and start every request statelessly
Use Content Safety categories as the memory store

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Azure AI Foundry Agent Service](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/overview).

**B** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry Agent Service](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/overview).

**C** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry Agent Service](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/overview).

**D** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Azure AI Foundry Agent Service](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/agents/overview).
